concepts.summary: 変更が起きた可能性のある発生元行を識別する変更検知履歴
Dirty Key がどの Transfer Run と転送先設定で処理され、どの結果になったかを記録する概念
転送先テーブルへ書き込むために必要な転送先側の仕様
転送元データソースを定義し、Destination への接続を管理する設定
Transfer Setting のデータソースを特定 Destination へ接続する宛先別設定
Transfer Execution が生成する実行引数記録兼プロセスヘッダー
Transfer Run をプロセスヘッダーとして生成し、Work Item に整理された情報をもとに転送表現を選ぶメインルーチン
Dirty Key を Transfer Setting の文脈で固定化し、転送判断を付与した作業対象
転送先行の転送元を特定し、元ネタを追跡する概念
転送済みの黒伝のうち、まだ取り消されていない現在有効な黒伝
mutable transfer model で既存の転送先行を物理削除する削除表現
転送元の現在値を転送先へコピーし、黒伝を作る通常転送
immutable transfer model で黒伝を反転した赤伝を追加する取消または訂正表現
lineage の説明用語。キー変換で lineage 管理を説明するための別名。
重複制御。Dirty Key では管理しない概念として登場する。
Black Transfer のうち、新しい黒伝を追加する転送表現。
Black Transfer のうち、mutable transfer model で既存の黒伝を直接 UPDATE する転送表現。

relationships.reason: Transfer Run targets exactly one Transfer Setting.
Transfer Run must not redefine Transfer Setting content.
Transfer Run must not redefine Dirty Key semantics.
Transfer Setting connects to Destination through Destination Link.
Transfer Setting manages destination-side connections as Destination Links.
Transfer Setting must not redefine Destination semantics.
Transfer Setting must not redefine Dirty Key semantics.
Destination Link belongs to exactly one Transfer Setting.
Destination Link connects the Transfer Setting data source to one Destination.
Destination Link mapping must not redefine Destination physical semantics.
Destination is an independent destination-side specification, not a child of Transfer Setting.
Destination must not change destination-side semantics for Transfer Setting-specific needs.
Dirty Key is not a transfer instruction, transfer state, or transfer result.
Lineage records which Transfer Run produced the destination row.
Lineage records which Transfer Setting the destination row was produced under.
Lineage records which Destination the destination row belongs to.
Key Map is a lineage explanation term, not a separate Concept Spec candidate.
Active Black may be used with Lineage when identifying the currently active black row in the immutable transfer model.
Lineage records destination-row origin, while Active Black identifies the currently active black row.
Active Black refers to a black row that exists on the destination side.
Black Transfer uses Active Black to avoid duplicate transfer and creates an Active Black when the resulting black row is current.
Black Transfer creates a black row on the destination side.
In the immutable transfer model, successful Black Transfer records the correspondence between the source data row and the black destination row as Lineage.
Black Transfer copies current source values to create a black row, while Red Transfer creates a reversing red row.
Red Transfer needs the Active Black that should be reversed.
Red Transfer relies on Destination transfer model and red-transfer column information.
Successful Red Transfer records the correspondence between the reversed existing destination row and the generated red destination row as Lineage.
Red Transfer adds the reversing red row, while Active Black identifies the currently active black row to reverse.
Transfer Execution creates Transfer Run as an execution argument record and process header.
Transfer Execution follows the Transfer Setting targeted by the Transfer Run.
Transfer Execution uses Destination transfer model and destination-side rules.
Transfer Execution consumes Dirty Key Management records as transfer candidates.
Transfer Execution uses Dirty Key Processing to exclude processed Dirty Keys before creating Work Items and records Dirty Key Processing after processing.
Transfer Execution fixes Dirty Key records as Work Items.
Transfer Execution uses Work Item decisions to choose black transfer, red transfer, physical delete, or no operation.
Transfer Execution uses Active Black to decide whether to black transfer, red transfer, update, delete, or do nothing.
Transfer Execution performs Black Transfer when current source values should be copied to the destination.
Transfer Execution performs Red Transfer in the immutable transfer model when an active black row must be reversed.
Work Item is created by fixing Dirty Key records as transfer work.
Work Item creation excludes Dirty Keys that already have Dirty Key Processing records in the same setting and destination-link context.
Work Item decisions are made in a Transfer Setting context.
Work Item decisions use the presence or absence of Active Black.
Work Item decisions use Destination transfer model.
Dirty Key is change-detection history, while Work Item is fixed transfer work with a transfer decision.
Work Item may be affected by lineage for duplicate, retransmission, or deletion-equivalent decisions.
Dirty Key Processing records processed Dirty Key IDs.
Dirty Key Processing records which Transfer Run processed the Dirty Key and what processing result was produced.
Dirty Key Processing is scoped by Transfer Setting.
Dirty Key Processing records destination setting ID because one Transfer Run can cover multiple Destination Links.
Dirty Key is immutable change-detection history, while Dirty Key Processing is a processed-state record.
Black Insert Transfer is the Black Transfer variant that inserts a new black row.
Black Update Transfer is the mutable Black Transfer variant that directly updates an existing black row.
Physical Delete Transfer requires the Active Black that should be physically deleted.
Physical Delete Transfer physically deletes an existing destination row.
Mutable delete is Physical Delete Transfer, while immutable delete is Red Transfer.
Transfer Execution uses Physical Delete Transfer in mutable transfer model delete cases.

relationshipKindNotes.reason: Needed to keep downgraded explanation terms machine-readable without treating them as independent Concept Specs.
